hook.text: A German company and a Japanese company report profits. Can you compare them? Not easily.

buildup.text: Every country had its own accounting rules, making cross-border investing a nightmare.

discovery.text: In 2001, the IASB was created to develop International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS).

twist.text: Over 140 countries now use IFRS. The U.S. still uses GAAP—the biggest holdout.

climax.text: The push for one global accounting language continues, but full convergence remains elusive.

punchline.text: One world, two rule books. The debate goes on.

quiz.question: How many countries currently use IFRS?
